Of course. Phoenix replaces four old systems with one cloud platform, and it is ninety percent built.
Good. Keep it short. What are the highlights?

The security review took longer. As a result, go-live moves to April. To summarize: faster, cheaper, delay under control. My recommendation is to approve April.

Hi, this is Rafael. I am recording a quick rehearsal of my Phoenix board presentation for Thursday, so I can hear how it sounds. Let me start with the overview. Phoenix replaces four old systems with one cloud platform, and it is now ninety percent built. The board is a busy audience, so I will keep the agenda to three items and put the key point first. Now the highlights. First, the pilot cut the average login time from eight seconds to two. In addition, the running cost is fifteen percent below the old budget. However, I want to be honest about one issue. The security review took longer than planned. As a result, the full go-live moves from March to early April. To summarize, the platform is faster, it is cheaper, and the one-month delay is under control. My recommendation is simple: approve the April go-live and release the final budget now. I will close with that single key point. I will keep my tone calm and formal, I will use connectors like however, in addition, and as a result to link my ideas, and I will leave the board feeling confident that the project is in safe hands.

Hi Rafael, this is Diana from the board, leaving you a short voicemail after your dry run. Overall, the brief is strong, and I want to give you three quick notes before Thursday. First, your overview is excellent. You put the key point first and you kept it to one paragraph, which is exactly what a busy board needs. Second, I liked that you were honest about the security delay instead of hiding it. When you say as a result the go-live moves to April, please add one sentence on how you are keeping that month under control, because the directors will ask. Third, your recommendation is clear, but say it twice: once at the start and once in your summary, so it is the last thing we hear. One small thing on delivery. On your second slide you speak very fast. Slow down, take a breath after each highlight, and let the numbers land. You sound most confident when you pause. Send me the final agenda the night before, and I will make sure you get your full ten minutes. Good work, Rafael, this is nearly there.
